title: nepeta natanzensis
intro: Nepeta natanzensis, also known as Natanz catmint, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the mint family, Lamiaceae. It is traditionally used in Iranian folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing anxiety and stress, improving digestion, and alleviating symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Nepeta natanzensis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its leaves and flowers can also be used fresh in salads or as a garnish for various dishes.
